creator: amdmi3@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Kalten from comment #1)

> Should we use this latest version on git://git.code.sf.net/p/xdiskusage/git
> 275754ad0a24716d86da1aaaf452b0906211e859 (solving other mistakes too)
> instead of patching in the ports tree?

We prefer not to use VCS snapshots. These may be broken and unstable, and we'll have to maintain and host the distfile ourselves. It's better to ask upstream to release a new version sooner.

> Should we set PORTVERSION=1.51.5 as it is the fifth fix since 1.51—if I am
> not mistaken?

No, PORTVERSION refers to upstream version, and there's no such thing as xdiskusage 1.51.5. We have PORTREVISION for this case (e.g. to mark a port update which does not involve switching to newer upstream version).
